Note
Demo
Study

Back Propagation ​

A Simple Toy ​

Introduction ​

ii:input
hi:hidden layer
oi:output
wi:weight
bi:bias

Learning Task

i1=0.05,i2=0.10
o1=0.01,o2=0.99

Learning is the process to get the right [w1,…,w8] to arrive at [o1=0.01,o2=0.99]

Algorithm Step ​

step 1: initialization of weights ​

set wi and bi to random values e.g.

[w1,w2,b1]=[0.15,0.20,0.35]
[w3,w4,b1]=[0.25,0.30,0.35]
[w5,w6,b2]=[0.40,0.45,0.60]
[w7,w8,b2]=[0.50,0.55,0.60]

step 2: forward pass ​

neth1=w1×i1+w2×i2+b1×1=0.3775
outh1=σ(neth1)=0.593269992
neth2=w3×i1+w4×i2+b1×1=0.3925
outh2=σ(neth2)=0.596884378

so that we have

neto1=w5×outh1+w6×outh2+b2×1=1.10590597
outo1=σ(neto1)=0.75136507
neto2=w7×outh1+w8×outh2+b2×1=1.2249214
outo2=σ(neto2)=0.772928465

step 3: calculate error ​

E=12∑i=12(outoi−oi)2

so that we have

Eo1=12(0.75136507−0.01)2=0.274811083
Eo2=12(0.772928465−0.99)2=0.023560026

step 4: backward pass ​

As Etotal is a function of wi and bi, we can use the chain rule to calculate the partial derivatives of Etotal with respect to wi and bi to see how to change each wi and bi. e.g.

∂Etotal∂w5=∂Etotal∂outo1×∂outo1∂neto1×∂neto1∂w5

Etotal=Eo1+Eo2=12(outo1−o1)2+12(outo2−o2)2

⇒∂Etotal∂outo1=outo1−o1

outo1=σ(neto1)=11+e−neto1

⇒∂outo1∂neto1=outo1×(1−outo1)

neto1=w5×outh1+w6×outh2+b2×1

⇒∂neto1∂w5=outh1

so that we have

∂Etotal∂w5=(outo1−o1)×outo1×(1−outo1)×outh1=0.082167041

To decrease the error, we then subtract this value from the current weight. But we need a parameter eta called learning rate.

w5+=w5−η×∂Etotal∂w5=0.35891648

Deeper Network and bias

∂Etotal∂w1=∂Eo1∂outo1×∂outo1∂w1+∂Eo2∂outo2×∂outo2∂w1=∂Eo1∂outo1×∂outo1∂neto1×∂neto1∂outh1×∂outh1∂neth1×∂neth1∂w1+∂Eo2∂outo2×∂outo2∂neto2×∂neto2∂outh1×∂outh1∂neth1×∂neth1∂w1=(outo1−o1)×outo1×(1−outo1)×w5×outh1×(1−outh1)×i1+(outo2−o2)×outo2×(1−outo2)××w7×outh1×(1−outh1)×i1∂Etotal∂b1=∂Eo1∂outo1×∂outo1∂b1+∂Eo2∂outo2×∂outo2∂b1=∂Eo1∂outo1×∂outo1∂neto1×(∂neto1∂outh1×∂outh1∂neth1×∂neth1∂b1+∂neto1∂outh2×∂outh2∂neth2×∂neth2∂b1)+∂Eo2∂outo2×∂outo2∂neto2×(∂neto2∂outh1×∂outh1∂neth1×∂neth1∂b1+∂neto2∂outh2×∂outh2∂neth2×∂neth2∂b1)=(outo1−o1)×outo1×(1−outo1)×(w5×outh1×(1−outh1)+w6×outh2×(1−outh2))+(outo2−o2)×outo2×(1−outo2)×(w7×outh1×(1−outh1)+w8×outh2×(1−outh2))

Finally, we get the new weights and biases

[w1+,w2+,b1+]=[0.149780716,0.19956143,0.3450265]
[w3+,w4+,b1+]=[0.24975114,0.29950229,0.3450265]
[w5+,w6+,b2+]=[0.35891648,0.408666186,0.511301270]
[w7+,w8+,b2+]=[0.511301270,0.561370121,0.511301270]

Linear Algebra

we can use matrix to simplify the calculation

  • the first layer:
[w1w2b1w3w4b1]×[i1i21]=[neth1neth2]
  • the second layer:
[w5w6b2w7w8b2]×[outh1outh21]=[neto1neto2]
  • net and out:
[outx1outx2]=σ([netx1netx2])
  • the error:
[Eo1Eo2]=12×([outo1outo2]−[o1o2])2
  • delta layer 2:
[δo1δo2]=[outo1−o1outo2−o2]∗[outo1outo2]∗(1−[outo1outo2])
  • dev layer 2:
[∂Etotal∂w5∂Etotal∂w6(∂Etotal∂b2)1∂Etotal∂w7∂Etotal∂w8(∂Etotal∂b2)2]=[δo1δo2]×[outh1outh21]T
  • delta layer 1:
[δh1δh2]=[w5w7w6w8]T×[δo1δo2]∗[outh1outh2]∗(1−[outh1outh2])
  • dev layer 1:
[∂Etotal∂w1∂Etotal∂w2(∂Etotal∂b1)1∂Etotal∂w3∂Etotal∂w4(∂Etotal∂b1)2]=[δh1δh2]×[i1i21]T
  • bias
∂Etotal∂bn=(∂Etotal∂bn)1+(∂Etotal∂bn)2

step 5: repeat step 2-4 ​

We can repeat step 2-4 until the error is small enough.

Code ​

WARNING

  • The python code is generated by copilot, it may not be correct.
  • Matlab's code randomly generate different bias, so the result has problem.
typescript
import * as tf from '@tensorflow/tfjs'
export function bpLearn() {
  function learn(inputArray: number[], outputArray: number[], stepNumber: number, learningLoopNumber: number) {
    return tf.tidy(() => {
      const input = tf.tensor(inputArray, [2, 1])
      const output = tf.tensor(outputArray, [2, 1])
      let Bias1Value = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([1, 1])).dataSync()[0]
      let Bias2Value = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([1, 1])).dataSync()[0]
      let Bias1 = tf.tensor([Bias1Value, Bias1Value], [2, 1])
      let Bias2 = tf.tensor([Bias2Value, Bias2Value], [2, 1])
      const layer1Parameter = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([2, 2]).concat(Bias1, 1))
      const layer2Parameter = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([2, 2]).concat(Bias2, 1))
      const learningLoop = learningLoopNumber
      const step = tf.tensor(stepNumber, [1, 1])
      const errors = Array.from({ length: learningLoop }, () => 0)
      const one = tf.tensor([1], [1, 1])
      for (let i = 0; i < learningLoop; i++) {
        const layer1Input = layer1Parameter.matMul(input.concat(one))
        const layer1Output = layer1Input.sigmoid()
        const layer2Input = layer2Parameter.matMul(layer1Output.concat(one))
        const layer2Output = layer2Input.sigmoid()
        errors[i] = layer2Output.sub(output).square().sum().dataSync()[0] / 2
        const deltaLayer2 = layer2Output.sub(output).mul(layer2Output).mul(layer2Output.sub(1).neg())
        let devLayer2 = deltaLayer2.matMul(layer1Output.concat(one).transpose())
        const deltaLayer1 = layer2Parameter.slice([0, 0], [2, 2]).transpose().matMul(deltaLayer2)
          .mul(layer1Output).mul(layer1Output.sub(1).neg())
        let devLayer1 = deltaLayer1.matMul(input.concat(one).transpose())
        Bias1Value = devLayer1.slice([0, 2], [2, 1]).sum().dataSync()[0]
        Bias2Value = devLayer2.slice([0, 2], [2, 1]).sum().dataSync()[0]
        Bias1 = tf.tensor([Bias1Value, Bias1Value], [2, 1])
        Bias2 = tf.tensor([Bias2Value, Bias2Value], [2, 1])
        devLayer1 = devLayer1.slice([0, 0], [2, 2]).concat(Bias1, 1)
        devLayer2 = devLayer2.slice([0, 0], [2, 2]).concat(Bias2, 1)
        layer2Parameter.assign(layer2Parameter.sub(step.mul(devLayer2)))
        layer1Parameter.assign(layer1Parameter.sub(step.mul(devLayer1)))
      }
      return { error: errors, layer1Parameter: layer1Parameter.arraySync(), layer2Parameter: layer2Parameter.arraySync() }
    })
  }
  return learn([0.05, 0.10], [0.01, 0.99], 0.5, 1000)
}
import * as tf from '@tensorflow/tfjs'
export function bpLearn() {
  function learn(inputArray: number[], outputArray: number[], stepNumber: number, learningLoopNumber: number) {
    return tf.tidy(() => {
      const input = tf.tensor(inputArray, [2, 1])
      const output = tf.tensor(outputArray, [2, 1])
      let Bias1Value = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([1, 1])).dataSync()[0]
      let Bias2Value = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([1, 1])).dataSync()[0]
      let Bias1 = tf.tensor([Bias1Value, Bias1Value], [2, 1])
      let Bias2 = tf.tensor([Bias2Value, Bias2Value], [2, 1])
      const layer1Parameter = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([2, 2]).concat(Bias1, 1))
      const layer2Parameter = tf.variable(tf.randomNormal([2, 2]).concat(Bias2, 1))
      const learningLoop = learningLoopNumber
      const step = tf.tensor(stepNumber, [1, 1])
      const errors = Array.from({ length: learningLoop }, () => 0)
      const one = tf.tensor([1], [1, 1])
      for (let i = 0; i < learningLoop; i++) {
        const layer1Input = layer1Parameter.matMul(input.concat(one))
        const layer1Output = layer1Input.sigmoid()
        const layer2Input = layer2Parameter.matMul(layer1Output.concat(one))
        const layer2Output = layer2Input.sigmoid()
        errors[i] = layer2Output.sub(output).square().sum().dataSync()[0] / 2
        const deltaLayer2 = layer2Output.sub(output).mul(layer2Output).mul(layer2Output.sub(1).neg())
        let devLayer2 = deltaLayer2.matMul(layer1Output.concat(one).transpose())
        const deltaLayer1 = layer2Parameter.slice([0, 0], [2, 2]).transpose().matMul(deltaLayer2)
          .mul(layer1Output).mul(layer1Output.sub(1).neg())
        let devLayer1 = deltaLayer1.matMul(input.concat(one).transpose())
        Bias1Value = devLayer1.slice([0, 2], [2, 1]).sum().dataSync()[0]
        Bias2Value = devLayer2.slice([0, 2], [2, 1]).sum().dataSync()[0]
        Bias1 = tf.tensor([Bias1Value, Bias1Value], [2, 1])
        Bias2 = tf.tensor([Bias2Value, Bias2Value], [2, 1])
        devLayer1 = devLayer1.slice([0, 0], [2, 2]).concat(Bias1, 1)
        devLayer2 = devLayer2.slice([0, 0], [2, 2]).concat(Bias2, 1)
        layer2Parameter.assign(layer2Parameter.sub(step.mul(devLayer2)))
        layer1Parameter.assign(layer1Parameter.sub(step.mul(devLayer1)))
      }
      return { error: errors, layer1Parameter: layer1Parameter.arraySync(), layer2Parameter: layer2Parameter.arraySync() }
    })
  }
  return learn([0.05, 0.10], [0.01, 0.99], 0.5, 1000)
}
python
import numpy as np

Input_gvn = np.array([[0.05], [0.1]])
Output_tgt = np.array([[0.01], [0.99]])

step = 0.5

layer1_para = np.array([[0.15, 0.2, 0.35], [0.25, 0.3, 0.35]]) 
layer2_para = np.array([[0.4, 0.45, 0.6], [0.5, 0.55, 0.6]]) 

layer1_para = np.random.normal(0, 1, (2, 3)) 
layer2_para = np.random.normal(0, 1, (2, 3)) 

node_func = lambda x: 1 / (1 + np.exp(-x))

learning_loop = 1000
mmse = []

for I in range(learning_loop):
    layer1_input = np.dot(layer1_para, np.concatenate((Input_gvn, np.array([[1]]))))
    layer1_output = node_func(layer1_input)
    layer2_input = np.dot(layer2_para, np.concatenate((layer1_output, np.array([[1]]))))
    layer2_output = node_func(layer2_input)

    layer2_error = 0.5 * np.power(layer2_output - Output_tgt, 2)
    layer2_delta = (layer2_output - Output_tgt) * layer2_output * (1 - layer2_output)

    layer1_error = 0.5 * np.power(layer1_output - Output_tgt, 2)
    layer1_delta = (layer1_output - Output_tgt) * layer1_output * (1 - layer1_output)

    layer2_dev = np.dot(layer2_delta, np.concatenate((layer1_output, np.array([[1]]))).transpose())
    layer1_dev = np.dot(layer1_delta, np.concatenate((Input_gvn, np.array([[1]]))).transpose())

    layer2_para = layer2_para - step * layer2_dev
    layer1_para = layer1_para - step * layer1_dev

    mmse.append(layer2_error[0][0])

print(layer1_para)
print(layer2_para)
print(mmse)
import numpy as np

Input_gvn = np.array([[0.05], [0.1]])
Output_tgt = np.array([[0.01], [0.99]])

step = 0.5

layer1_para = np.array([[0.15, 0.2, 0.35], [0.25, 0.3, 0.35]]) 
layer2_para = np.array([[0.4, 0.45, 0.6], [0.5, 0.55, 0.6]]) 

layer1_para = np.random.normal(0, 1, (2, 3)) 
layer2_para = np.random.normal(0, 1, (2, 3)) 

node_func = lambda x: 1 / (1 + np.exp(-x))

learning_loop = 1000
mmse = []

for I in range(learning_loop):
    layer1_input = np.dot(layer1_para, np.concatenate((Input_gvn, np.array([[1]]))))
    layer1_output = node_func(layer1_input)
    layer2_input = np.dot(layer2_para, np.concatenate((layer1_output, np.array([[1]]))))
    layer2_output = node_func(layer2_input)

    layer2_error = 0.5 * np.power(layer2_output - Output_tgt, 2)
    layer2_delta = (layer2_output - Output_tgt) * layer2_output * (1 - layer2_output)

    layer1_error = 0.5 * np.power(layer1_output - Output_tgt, 2)
    layer1_delta = (layer1_output - Output_tgt) * layer1_output * (1 - layer1_output)

    layer2_dev = np.dot(layer2_delta, np.concatenate((layer1_output, np.array([[1]]))).transpose())
    layer1_dev = np.dot(layer1_delta, np.concatenate((Input_gvn, np.array([[1]]))).transpose())

    layer2_para = layer2_para - step * layer2_dev
    layer1_para = layer1_para - step * layer1_dev

    mmse.append(layer2_error[0][0])

print(layer1_para)
print(layer2_para)
print(mmse)
matlab
clc;
clear;

Input_gvn = [0.05; 0.1];
Output_tgt = [0.01; 0.99];

step = 0.5;

layer1_para=[0.15,0.2,0.35;0.25,0.30,0.35]; 
layer2_para=[0.40,0.45,0.60;0.50,0.55,0.6]; 

layer1_para = rand(2, 3); 
layer2_para = rand(2, 3); 

node_func = @(x) 1 ./ (1 + exp(-x));

learning_loop = 1000;
mmse = zeros(1, learning_loop);

for I = 1:learning_loop
    layer1_input = layer1_para * [Input_gvn; 1];
    layer1_output = node_func(layer1_input);
    layer2_input = layer2_para * [layer1_output; 1];
    layer2_output = node_func(layer2_input);

    mmse(I) = 0.5 * sum((layer2_output - Output_tgt) .^ 2);

    delta_layer2 = (layer2_output - Output_tgt) .* layer2_output .* (1 - layer2_output);
    dev_layer2 = delta_layer2 * layer1_output';
    delta_layer1 = layer2_para(:, 1:2)' * delta_layer2 .* layer1_output .* (1 - layer1_output);
    dev_layer1 = delta_layer1 * Input_gvn';

    layer2_para(:, 1:2) = layer2_para(:, 1:2) - step * dev_layer2;
    layer1_para(:, 1:2) = layer1_para(:, 1:2) - step * dev_layer1;
end

plot(1:10:learning_loop, mmse(1:10:end), '-ro');
xlabel('learning round');
ylabel('total error (MMSE)');
grid on
clc;
clear;

Input_gvn = [0.05; 0.1];
Output_tgt = [0.01; 0.99];

step = 0.5;

layer1_para=[0.15,0.2,0.35;0.25,0.30,0.35]; 
layer2_para=[0.40,0.45,0.60;0.50,0.55,0.6]; 

layer1_para = rand(2, 3); 
layer2_para = rand(2, 3); 

node_func = @(x) 1 ./ (1 + exp(-x));

learning_loop = 1000;
mmse = zeros(1, learning_loop);

for I = 1:learning_loop
    layer1_input = layer1_para * [Input_gvn; 1];
    layer1_output = node_func(layer1_input);
    layer2_input = layer2_para * [layer1_output; 1];
    layer2_output = node_func(layer2_input);

    mmse(I) = 0.5 * sum((layer2_output - Output_tgt) .^ 2);

    delta_layer2 = (layer2_output - Output_tgt) .* layer2_output .* (1 - layer2_output);
    dev_layer2 = delta_layer2 * layer1_output';
    delta_layer1 = layer2_para(:, 1:2)' * delta_layer2 .* layer1_output .* (1 - layer1_output);
    dev_layer1 = delta_layer1 * Input_gvn';

    layer2_para(:, 1:2) = layer2_para(:, 1:2) - step * dev_layer2;
    layer1_para(:, 1:2) = layer1_para(:, 1:2) - step * dev_layer1;
end

plot(1:10:learning_loop, mmse(1:10:end), '-ro');
xlabel('learning round');
ylabel('total error (MMSE)');
grid on
